reasoning: By May 27, just 11 days after the May 16 jailbreak, 8 of the 10 escaped inmates had been recaptured. Only two remain at large (Antoine Massey and Derrick Groves) as of May 30. Law enforcement has aggressively dismantled their support network, arresting at least 14 people for aiding the escapees. Additionally, the reward for information was recently increased to $50,000 per escapee, a massive incentive that has already generated nearly 700 tips and led to active sightings (e.g., in Natchez, MS). While Massey has a history of evading capture for over two weeks in 2019, the current manhunt is much better funded, coordinated, and publicized. With 31 days left until the July 1 deadline, the resources dedicated to catching just two remaining individuals make it highly probable (>90%) that both will be apprehended or otherwise neutralized before the resolution date.
